name: Chris Koster
subject: Back to business
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Koster for Missouri, Gary Mallory, Treasurer
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/1957499685757650944/-804597819569207296?utm_campaign=engg_biz_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=koster&ms=em16160524enggbizgqfulck&refcode=em16160524enggbizgqfulck)

Dear Friend,

The Missouri General Assembly has wasted another legislative session playing political games.

Missouri families and businesses deserve better than this.

Instead of focusing on attacking workers, restricting women's rights, and enshrining discrimination into our constitution, we need our legislature to work together to promote business in our state. GOP leaders in the General Assembly have gone three years without a comprehensive economic development proposal -- on my watch, Jefferson City will get back to business.

**[Watch my new video and sign my petition telling the legislature to get serious about promoting economic development in Missouri. ](https://act.myngp.com/el/1957499685757650944/-804597819569207296?utm_campaign=engg_biz_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=koster&ms=em16160524enggbizgqfulck&refcode=em16160524enggbizgqfulck)**

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/1957499685757650944/-804597819569207296?utm_campaign=engg_biz_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=koster&ms=em16160524enggbizgqfulck&refcode=em16160524enggbizgqfulck)

As Governor, I will work with the legislature to put forward a comprehensive economic development bill every year that creates real opportunities for Missourians. **[Join my fight to build a sustainable economic climate for future generations. ](https://act.myngp.com/el/1957499685757650944/-804597819569207296?utm_campaign=engg_biz_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=koster&ms=em16160524enggbizgqfulck&refcode=em16160524enggbizgqfulck)**

Together, we can end the combative climate in Jefferson City and build an economy that works for all Missourians.

Thank you,

Chris Koster

[DONATE](https://act.myngp.com/el/1957499685757650944/-660482631493351424?utm_campaign=fnd_b2b_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=koster&refcode=em16_160524_fnd_gq_b2b_ful__ck)
Paid for by Koster for Missouri, Gary Mallory, Treasurer
---

Jefferson City MO 65102 United States

name: Josh Orton, RussFeingold.com
subject: Voter ID challenged
disclaimer_text: paid for by Russ for Wisconsin, Joe Sensenbrenner, Madison, WI, Treasurer
body:
Wisconsin's voter ID law is among the nation's harshest. | [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf42aa/1c469400/56cc189/356478a/2011213237/VEsH/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/voterID?utm_source=ema_2016-05-24_voterID_OOS&source=ema_2016-05-24_voterID_OOS&utm_campaign=voterID&utm_medium=ema") Friend, Right now, Governor Scott Walker's voter ID law is being challenged in federal court. (Yes, the same Scott Walker who somehow managed to blow $90,000 A DAY on his short-lived, failed bid for president just this past fall.) We're seeing more and more of these discriminatory laws being rammed through Republican legislatures across the country, and the one we have here in Wisconsin is among the harshest. It restricted early voting hours, and changed the laws regarding absentee ballots, putting more obstacles in place to voting. And it disproportionately affects people of color and young people. **[We should be making it easier for every eligible voter to cast their ballots, not harder. Add your name if you agree.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf42aa/1c469400/56cc189/356478a/2011213237/VEsE/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/voterID?utm_source=ema_2016-05-24_voterID_OOS&source=ema_2016-05-24_voterID_OOS&utm_campaign=voterID&utm_medium=ema")** [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf42aa/1c469400/56cc189/356478a/2011213237/VEsF/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/voterID?utm_source=ema_2016-05-24_voterID_OOS&source=ema_2016-05-24_voterID_OOS&utm_campaign=voterID&utm_medium=ema") [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf42aa/1c469400/56cc189/356478a/2011213237/VEsC/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/voterID?utm_source=ema_2016-05-24_voterID_OOS&source=ema_2016-05-24_voterID_OOS&utm_campaign=voterID&utm_medium=ema") Republicans know what they're doing when they pass these laws -- you saw it when Republican Congressman Glenn Grothman said, and I quote, "Now we have photo ID, and I think photo ID is gonna make a little bit of a difference." You can't make this stuff up, folks: They're not even trying to hide it anymore. Voter ID is fully intended to "make a difference" in elections -- a partisan difference in favor of Republicans. **[Help us send a message: Add your name to take a stand against voter suppression.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf42aa/1c469400/56cc189/356478a/2011213237/VEsD/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/voterID?utm_source=ema_2016-05-24_voterID_OOS&source=ema_2016-05-24_voterID_OOS&utm_campaign=voterID&utm_medium=ema")** It's up to us to make our voices heard, and show Republican legislators that we won't stand for this. We're hopeful that the federal court will strike down this law here in Wisconsin, but we need you to help us make a stand -- there are so many of these laws all over the country that must be challenged as well. They go against the very heart of what our democracy -- and our country -- stands for. Thanks, Josh Orton
Senior Advisor | [ADD YOUR NAME »](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf42aa/1c469400/56cc189/356478a/2011213237/VEsA/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/voterID?utm_source=ema_2016-05-24_voterID_OOS&source=ema_2016-05-24_voterID_OOS&utm_campaign=voterID&utm_medium=ema")

clean_body: Wisconsin's voter ID law is among the nation's harshest. | "" Friend, Right now, Governor Scott Walker's voter ID law is being challenged in federal court. (Yes, the same Scott Walker who somehow managed to blow $90,000 A DAY on his short-lived, failed bid for president just this past fall.) We're seeing more and more of these discriminatory laws being rammed through Republican legislatures across the country, and the one we have here in Wisconsin is among the harshest. It restricted early voting hours, and changed the laws regarding absentee ballots, putting more obstacles in place to voting. And it disproportionately affects people of color and young people. "" "" "" Republicans know what they're doing when they pass these laws -- you saw it when Republican Congressman Glenn Grothman said, and I quote, "Now we have photo ID, and I think photo ID is gonna make a little bit of a difference." You can't make this stuff up, folks: They're not even trying to hide it anymore. Voter ID is fully intended to "make a difference" in elections -- a partisan difference in favor of Republicans. "" It's up to us to make our voices heard, and show Republican legislators that we won't stand for this. We're hopeful that the federal court will strike down this law here in Wisconsin, but we need you to help us make a stand -- there are so many of these laws all over the country that must be challenged as well. They go against the very heart of what our democracy -- and our country -- stands for. Thanks, Josh Orton Senior Advisor | ""

With all of the money coming in, it's no wonder Kelly Ayotte has spent the last three months refusing to do her job by obstructing the Supreme Court confirmation process. She's kowtowing to her party leadership and corporate special interests, and she's gambling with justice for Granite Staters.
Maggie knows we have to secure the future of the Supreme Court, and get the Senate working again for New Hampshire. She'll be a Senator who fights for hard-working families from Pittsburg to Nashua, and she's lucky to have you on her team.

name: Ariana Davis, JayInslee.com
subject: Attacking income inequality
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
Jay Inslee is fighting for the 99 percent. [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=ODk2MTE4NQS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTIyMjcwNjQzS0&mt=1&rt=0)

| Dear Friend -- As a grocery store worker in Renton, I know the income inequality gap is widening and getting worse. I see families every day that are struggling to pay for groceries and to have enough to eat. Working families in Washington, like mine, are fighting to get by. We can talk about this issue as much as we want, but action is what counts. We need bold, progressive action that gets results. Governor Inslee is acting. He's fighting to raise the minimum wage and guarantee paid sick leave for workers in Washington. Not only that, he's taking on obscene CEO pay as well. When CEOs got massive compensation packages while paying their workers cut-rate wages, Governor Inslee has asked the state investment board to use their proxy votes to reject their unfair compensation. [**We need to protect our allies like Governor Inslee. Will you join Jay 's fight for working families by adding your name today? **](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=ODk2MTE4NQS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTIyMjcwNjQzS0&mt=1&rt=0) Working families like mine have an ally and an advocate in Governor Inslee. He knows that in the fight for income equality there's no silver bullet -- raising the wage, guaranteeing sick leave for every Washington worker, passing tax credits for working-class families, making college tuition affordable, and taking on obscene CEO pay -- it all has to be part of the solution. And to get it done, Washington families need a governor who is working for them -- that's Governor Inslee. The millionaires, billionaires, and special interests might not like it, but they already have enough advocates in power. The fight of our time is to level the playing field for working people across Washington state. That's why I'm so proud to stand with Governor Inslee. [**I 'm proud to support Governor Inslee. Will you lend your support to the governor's re-election campaign by adding your name in support today? **](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=ODk2MTE4NQS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTIyMjcwNjQzS0&mt=1&rt=0) Thank you. In solidarity, Ariana Davis

name: VanHollen.org
subject: no summer vacation
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
|
Have you had a chance to join thousands in calling for Senate Republicans to end their obstructionism and take action on the president's Supreme Court nominee?

[**Chris has demanded that the Senate skip its summer vacation if it fails to act on the Garland nomination by the end of July. Will you take a minute to sign your name in solidarity? >>**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91d1b/70cdc2ba/47ef9cf4/396d73d/3535067965/VEsH/ "http://go.democrataction.com/page/s/scotus-act?source=cvh&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+Chris+has+demanded+that+the+Senate+skip+&utm_campaign=em052416")

Thank you for making your voice heard.

Begin forwarded message:
>
> Friend,
>
> Over the last few months, it's been shocking to see how unapologetically Mitch McConnell and other Republicans have declared their intention not to consider the President's nominee for the Supreme Court.
>
> Frankly, it is unacceptable. That's why I, along with a group of my colleagues, just introduced legislation requiring Congress to remain in session through the summer recess unless the Senate holds confirmation hearings for a nominee by July 19th.
>
> **[Please add your name now to tell Republicans in Congress: There will be no summer vacation until you do your jobs!](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91d1b/70cdc2ba/47ef9cf4/396d73a/3535067965/VEsE/ "http://go.democrataction.com/page/s/scotus-act?source=cvh&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+Please+add+your+name+now+to+tell+Republi&utm_campaign=em052416")**
>
> July 19th will mark 125 days since the President nominated U.S. Circuit Judge Merrick B. Garland for the Supreme Court -- which would be the longest period a Supreme Court nominee has ever had to wait for a hearing in U.S. history.
>
> **Here are the facts:** Republicans in the Senate know that Judge Garland is a qualified candidate for Supreme Court, but they're continuing to play politics with the highest court in the land.
>
> That's not a decision we can afford. And not the type of service to our country that Americans deserve. We must come together and make our government work for all its citizens. But if we don't make our voices heard today, nothing will change.
>

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: I'm endorsing 8 more candidates
body:
#
Sisters and Brothers -
No president, not Bernie Sanders, not the greatest president you could possibly imagine, can take on the billionaire class alone. And that's because change never happens from the top down, it always occurs from the bottom up.
That's why today I am announcing my support for eight progressive state legislative candidates who embody the spirit of our political revolution.
**Today, they are state representatives running for re-election or challenging to win their first race. Tomorrow, they'll be progressives running for Congress and maybe even the presidency.** And in 2020, they'll help determine how congressional districts are drawn for the remainder of the next decade -- a key to Democrats taking back and holding the House of Representatives for the decade to come.
It's up to us to transform the future of the Democratic Party, and that's why I am proud to ask:
[**Can I count on you to split a $2.70 contribution to our campaign and a great slate of progressive state legislative candidates as a way of saying we are going to fight to transform our country, and the Democratic Party, from the bottom up?**](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bernie-for-state-candidates?refcode=em160524-s1-full "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bernie-for-state-candidates?refcode=em160524-s1-full")
**Justin Bamberg** is a state representative from South Carolina who was an early endorser of Hillary Clinton's campaign but switched his endorsement before the primary because of Bernie's work for "racial, social, and economic justice." Justin won his last election by ONLY 113 votes and needs our support in what's sure to be another close race this November.
**David Bowen** is the son of Jamaican immigrants who is running for his second term in the Wisconsin State Legislature. He fought for and won a living wage for Milwaukee city employees and is a progressive champion Wisconsin needs.
**Clara Hart** is a refugee from Mozambique whose family fled from violence when she was just 8 years old. Now she's running for the South Dakota House, where she hopes to continue her work for immigrant families.
**Terry Alexander** is a representative in the South Carolina legislature who supports raising the minimum wage, expanding health care, and protecting the right to vote. He is a courageous legislator who I'm proud to support.
**Carol Ammons** became the first African-American woman to win election in her district of the Illinois State Legislature, scoring an upset election victory against a well-funded establishment opponent. She's fighting for progressive issues and candidates and deserves our support.
**Chris Pearson** is running for the Vermont State Senate after spending four terms in the Vermont House of Representatives. He is a champion on the issues of livable wages, strong labor union advocacy, climate change, and voting rights. This year he was able to pass a bill into law for automatic voter registration. Chris is a good friend of working people and of mine.
**Jane Kim** is the first Korean American to win election in San Francisco, and she's running to become a California state senator. The daughter of immigrants, Jane is a civil rights attorney who's fought for affordable housing and fair wages in her city.
**Joe Salazar** is a representative to Colorado's state house who is a tireless advocate for stopping fracking, protecting civil rights, and advancing criminal justice reform. He won his last election by just 221 votes, and your support will go a long way to helping him win this year.
[**Can you split a $2.70 contribution to our campaign and these candidates as a way of saying we are going to fight to transform our country, and the Democratic Party, from top to bottom?**](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bernie-for-state-candidates?refcode=em160524-s1-full "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bernie-for-state-candidates?refcode=em160524-s1-full")
We are going to keep fighting for every vote, every delegate, and every state, but this moment also requires helping progressives who are fighting for change at the local level. That is the political revolution, and that's why splitting your contribution between our campaign and this great slate of local candidates is so important.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders
**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bernie-for-state-candidates?refcode=em160524-s1-full "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bernie-for-state-candidates?refcode=em160524-s1-full")**

Paid for by Bernie 2016
PO Box 905 - Burlington VT 05402 United States - (855) 4-BERNIE
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you need to update or change your information or email address, click here to [update your info](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/change-your-information?source=email_footer "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/change-your-information?source=email_footer"). Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can let us know if you'd like to [receive fewer emails](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20902 "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20902"). We'd hate to see you go, but if you need to do so, click here to [unsubscribe](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/unsubscribe/ "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/unsubscribe/"). Stand against the powerful special interests who are systematically buying our Congress and have their sights set on the presidency by [contributing to Bernie here](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/lets-go-bernie?refcode=email_footer "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/lets-go-bernie?refcode=email_footer").

It's simple: Our democracy should not be up for sale to the highest bidder. "" As Governor, Maggie signed common-sense bipartisan legislation to improve transparency in our elections. But, she knows we have to do more. We need you to join leaders like Maggie and call for real campaign finance reform that restores fairness and transparency to our elections system. "" -- Team Maggie ""
